- '[[openalex-2606.10762-amortized-simulation-based-inference-of-colliding-wind-binar]]'
title: Bridging Simulation-Observation Gap
---

**Background:** Amortized simulation-based inference (SBI) is typically trained on synthetic data generated by the same physical simulator used for the inference process.

**Question / Future Work:** There exists a persistent simulator-observation gap between synthetic data and real-world astronomical observations, which include factors such as stellar radiation fields, dust absorption, instrumental point-spread functions, and intrinsic stellar variability. Research is needed to develop robust neural posterior estimation (NPE) strategies that can account for this gap or perform reliable amortized model selection when applied to actual observational data.

**Why It Matters:** Bridging the gap between synthetic simulation-based training and real-world astronomical data is the primary barrier to the practical application of SBI in observational astrophysics.

**Evidence:** All experiments use synthetic data generated by the same simulator that trains the network. A real-world deployment would require addressing the inevitable simulator-observation gap.
